sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Killed) FROM perpetrator) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'China'
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Retrieve the name, height, and weight of perpetrators who have killed more than the average number of victims, excluding any from China.
schema:
Database: perpetrator

Table: perpetrator
Columns: Perpetrator_ID, People_ID, Date, Year, Location, Country, Killed, Injured
Sample Rows (Limit 3):
- (1, 1, '04.26 April 26/27', 1982.0, 'Uiryeong', 'South Korea', 56, 37)
- (2, 3, '11.18 Nov. 18', 1995.0, 'Zhaodong', 'China', 32, 16)
- (3, 4, '05.21 May 21', 1938.0, 'Kaio', 'Japan', 30, 3)

Table: people
Columns: People_ID, Name, Height, Weight, Home Town

- (3, 'Tyrone Brayan', 6.7, 220.0, 'Placentia, CA')
nl_question: Which perpetrators, excluding those from China, have significantly higher casualty rates than the average, warranting closer examination of their profiles?

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary)
FROM instructor AS T1
WHERE T1.dept_name IN (
  SELECT T2.dept_name
  FROM course AS T2
  WHERE T2.credits > 3
  GROUP BY T2.dept_name
  HAVING count(*) > 1

SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Calculate the total purchase value from each supplier, considering only products that were part of completed customer orders.

sql: SELECT T4.first_name, T4.last_name, T3.visit_start_datetime FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id WHERE T1.asset_details = 'dell laptop3' AND T2.fault_description = 'system error';

sql: SELECT p_aff.Name AS Winner_Name, p_aff.Party AS Winner_Party, d.Date FROM debate_people AS dp JOIN people AS p_aff ON dp.Affirmative = p_aff.People_ID JOIN debate AS d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T' UNION SELECT p_neg.Name, p_neg.Party, d.Date FROM debate_people AS dp JOIN people AS p_neg ON dp.Negative = p_neg.People_ID JOIN debate AS d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F';

sql: WITH PartyWins AS (SELECT p.Party, SUM(CASE WHEN dp.Affirmative = p.People_ID AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T' THEN 1 WHEN dp.Negative = p.People_ID AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Wins FROM people p JOIN debate_people dp ON p.People_ID = dp.Affirmative OR p.People_ID = dp.Negative GROUP BY p.Party) SELECT Party, Wins FROM PartyWins WHERE Wins = (SELECT MAX(Wins) FROM PartyWins);
result_summary: Retrieve the political party that has the highest number of debate wins, along with their total win count.

sql: SELECT A.Title AS AlbumTitle, T.Name AS TrackName, T.Milliseconds, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T.AlbumId ORDER BY T.Milliseconds DESC) AS LengthRank FROM Track AS T JOIN Album AS A ON T.AlbumId = A.AlbumId WHERE A.ArtistId = (SELECT ArtistId FROM Artist WHERE Name = 'Accept');
result_summary: Fetch the album title, track name, and length for all tracks by the artist 'Accept', ranking each track by its length from longest to shortest within its respective album.

sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, i.InvoiceId, i.Total, (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM Invoice WHERE CustomerId = c.CustomerId) AS CustomerAverageInvoice FROM Customer AS c JOIN Invoice AS i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.Country = 'Germany' ORDER BY c.CustomerId, i.InvoiceDate;
result_summary: This query fetches the first name, last name, invoice ID, and total for each invoice, alongside the customer's overall average invoice total, specifically for customers in Germany, ordered by customer and then by invoice date.

sql: SELECT Genre.Name, COUNT(Track.TrackId) AS NumberOfTracks FROM Genre JOIN Track ON Genre.GenreId = Track.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine ON Track.TrackId = InvoiceLine.TrackId GROUP BY Genre.Name HAVING COUNT(Track.TrackId) > 100 ORDER BY NumberOfTracks DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each music genre and the count of its sold tracks for genres with more than 100 sales, ordered by the track count in descending order.

sql: WITH CustomerSpending AS (SELECT CustomerId, SUM(Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId) SELECT C.FirstName, C.LastName, CS.TotalSpent FROM Customer C JOIN CustomerSpending CS ON C.CustomerId = CS.CustomerId WHERE CS.TotalSpent > (SELECT AVG(TotalSpent) FROM CustomerSpending) AND C.Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and total spending for customers from the USA whose spending is greater than the average spending of all customers.

sql: SELECT C.Country, G.Name as TopGenre, MAX(T.PurchaseCount) AS Purchases FROM (SELECT IL.TrackId, COUNT(*) AS PurchaseCount FROM InvoiceLine IL GROUP BY IL.TrackId) T JOIN Track TR ON T.TrackId = TR.TrackId JOIN Genre G ON TR.GenreId = G.GenreId JOIN Invoice I ON T.TrackId = (SELECT TrackId FROM InvoiceLine WHERE InvoiceId = I.InvoiceId LIMIT 1) JOIN Customer C ON I.CustomerId = C.CustomerId GROUP BY C.Country, G.Name ORDER BY C.Country, Purchases DESC;

sql:
SELECT T.Region_name FROM (SELECT T1.Region_id, T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Number_Deaths) AS total_deaths FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_id) AS T WHERE T.total_deaths > (SELECT AVG(Number_Deaths) FROM storm)
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Number_city_affected > 10.0

sql:
SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Race_ID)
FROM track AS T1
JOIN race AS T2
  ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID
GROUP BY
  T1.Track_ID
HAVING
  COUNT(T2.Race_ID) > (
    SELECT
      AVG(Race_Count)
    FROM (
      SELECT
        COUNT(Race_ID) AS Race_Count
      FROM race
      GROUP BY
        Track_ID
    ) AS T3
  );
result_summary: Return the names of tracks and the number of races they've hosted, but only for those tracks with a race count higher than the overall average number of races per track.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.fault_status = 'Waiting' THEN 1 END) AS waiting_visits, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.fault_status = 'Return' THEN 1 END) AS return_visits FROM Engineer_Visits AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id WHERE T1.engineer_id = 15 GROUP BY T2.first_name, T2.last_name;
result_summary: Retrieve the first name and last name for the maintenance engineer with ID 15, along with the total number of their visits that have a 'Waiting' and 'Return' fault status.

sql: SELECT b.breed_name, TotalCost FROM Breeds b JOIN (SELECT d.breed_code, SUM(t.cost_of_treatment) AS TotalCost FROM Dogs d JOIN Treatments t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id GROUP BY d.breed_code) AS BreedTotalCosts ON b.breed_code = BreedTotalCosts.breed_code ORDER BY TotalCost DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT A.Name AS ArtistName, COUNT(DISTINCT G.GenreId) AS NumberOfGenres FROM Artist A JOIN Album AL ON A.ArtistId = AL.ArtistId JOIN Track T ON AL.AlbumId = T.AlbumId JOIN Genre G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId GROUP BY A.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT G.GenreId) > 1 ORDER BY NumberOfGenres DESC;

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary)
FROM instructor AS T1
WHERE T1.dept_name IN (
  SELECT T2.dept_name
  FROM course AS T2
  WHERE T2.credits > 3
  GROUP BY T2.dept_name
  HAVING count(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY T1.dept_name;

sql:

  T1.department_name,
  COUNT(T2.staff_id)
FROM Departments AS T1
JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2
  ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id
WHERE
  T2.job_title_code = 'Sales Person'
GROUP BY
  T1.department_name;

sql:
SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_type_code = 'Clothes'
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T5.supplier_name = 'Tesco'

sql: WITH CompanyEngineers AS (SELECT T1.engineer_id FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.company_name = 'Langworth-Funk') SELECT T5.first_name, T5.last_name, T1.asset_details, T3.visit_start_datetime FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN CompanyEngineers AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T5 ON T4.engineer_id = T5.engineer_id;

sql: WITH MonthlyTemps AS (SELECT City_ID, 'Jan' as Month, Jan as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Feb' as Month, Feb as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Jul' as Month, Jul as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Dec' as Month, Dec as Temp FROM temperature) SELECT T2.City, T1.Month, T1.Temp FROM MonthlyTemps AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.Temp = (SELECT MAX(Temp) FROM MonthlyTemps WHERE City_ID = T1.City_ID) AND T1.City_ID = 2;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name, T3.course_count FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.id, COUNT(T2.course_id) AS course_count FROM teaches AS T2 GROUP BY T2.id HAVING COUNT(T2.course_id) > 1) AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.id WHERE T1.dept_name IN (SELECT dept_name FROM department WHERE budget > 200000)

sql: WITH AuthorPaperCounts AS (SELECT instID, authID, COUNT(paperID) as paper_count FROM Authorship GROUP BY instID, authID), RankedAuthors AS (SELECT instID, authID, paper_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY instID ORDER BY paper_count DESC) as rn FROM AuthorPaperCounts) SELECT T3.name, T2.fname, T2.lname, T1.paper_count FROM RankedAuthors AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.rn = 1;

sql:
SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Damage_millions_USD)
FROM region AS T1
JOIN affected_region AS T2
  ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id
JOIN storm AS T3
  ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID
GROUP BY
  T1.Region_name;

sql:
SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname
FROM (
  SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 1
  INTERSECT
  SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 2
) AS T1
JOIN Authors AS T2
  ON T1.authID = T2.authID;

sql: WITH StudentAttendanceCount AS (SELECT student_id, COUNT(course_id) AS attendance_count FROM Student_Course_Attendance GROUP BY student_id) SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.city, COALESCE(sac.attendance_count, 0) AS total_attendance FROM People AS p JOIN People_Addresses AS pa ON p.person_id = pa.person_id JOIN Addresses AS a ON pa.address_id = a.address_id LEFT JOIN StudentAttendanceCount AS sac ON p.person_id = sac.student_id WHERE pa.date_to > '2018-03-01' AND p.person_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students);
result_summary: Fetch the name, city, and total course attendance for students with a valid address after March 1, 2018.

sql: WITH StatisticsStudents AS (SELECT scr.student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations scr WHERE scr.course_id = '301'), PassedCandidates AS (SELECT ca.candidate_id FROM Candidate_Assessments ca WHERE ca.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass') SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.city, a.state_province_county FROM People p JOIN StatisticsStudents ss ON p.person_id = ss.student_id JOIN PassedCandidates pc ON p.person_id = pc.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses pa ON p.person_id = pa.person_id JOIN Addresses a ON pa.address_id = a.address_id WHERE pa.date_to > '2018-01-01';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name and address of people who are students registered for the statistics course and have passed an assessment, using only address information that is valid after January 1st, 2018.

sql: SELECT d.District_name, SUM(CASE WHEN s.Ranking = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS top_tier_stores, SUM(CASE WHEN s.Ranking = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS mid_tier_stores, SUM(CASE WHEN s.Ranking > 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS other_stores FROM store s JOIN store_district sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID JOIN district d ON sd.District_ID = d.District_ID GROUP BY d.District_name;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Country FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT company_id FROM flight GROUP BY company_id HAVING count(*) >= 1) EXCEPT SELECT T1.name, T3.Country FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Incorporated_in = 'China'

sql: SELECT T1.Model, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID WHERE T2.Pilot_ID IN (SELECT Pilot_ID FROM pilot WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM pilot)) GROUP BY T1.Model ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Record_ID) DESC

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, sum(T2.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id  =  T2.supplier_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id  =  T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_type_code  =  'Clothes' GROUP BY T1.supplier_name HAVING sum(T2.total_value_purchased)  >  (SELECT sum(T5.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T4 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id  =  T5.supplier_id WHERE T4.supplier_name  =  'Tesco')

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color, LEAD(product, 1, 'N/A') OVER (ORDER BY pages_per_minute_color ASC) as next_fastest_product FROM product WHERE max_page_size = 'A4' AND dpi = 600;
result_summary: Fetch the product name, its color pages-per-minute, and the name of the next fastest product for all products that support A4 paper and have a 600 DPI resolution, ordered by speed.

sql: WITH DepartureCounts AS (SELECT al.Airline AS AirlineName, ap.City, COUNT(f.FlightNo) AS NumDepartures, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY al.Airline ORDER BY COUNT(f.FlightNo) DESC) AS RankNum FROM flights AS f JOIN airlines AS al ON f.Airline = al.uid JOIN airports AS ap ON TRIM(f.SourceAirport) = ap.AirportCode WHERE al.Country = 'USA' GROUP BY al.Airline, ap.City) SELECT AirlineName, TRIM(City) AS TopDepartureCity, NumDepartures FROM DepartureCounts WHERE RankNum = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the top departure city and its corresponding number of departures for each individual airline in the USA.

nl_question: List all track names, their genre, and media type for tracks identified as 'Rock' genre and 'MPEG audio file' format.

sql: SELECT P.Name AS PlaylistName, MT.Name AS MediaTypeName, COUNT(T.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Playlist P JOIN PlaylistTrack PT ON P.PlaylistId = PT.PlaylistId JOIN Track T ON PT.TrackId = T.TrackId JOIN MediaType MT ON T.MediaTypeId = MT.MediaTypeId GROUP BY P.Name, MT.Name ORDER BY P.Name, TrackCount DESC;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the count of tracks for each media type within every playlist, ordered by playlist name and then by the track count in descending order.
